title: Arrays, Dynamic Sequences, and Locality · Complexity Checkpoint
summary: Design an event buffer with predictable indexing, insertion, and memory behavior. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an array-backed sequence with operation traces. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain indexing and cache-local traversal from contiguous logical storage.
Trace capacity growth and amortized append behavior through resize events.
Compare indexed access, search, middle insertion, deletion, slicing, and copying costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Contiguous Layout correctness-and-cost proof for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Contiguous Layout assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use the sequence [6, 2, 6, 1, 9, 3]. Preserve duplicate policy, identify the boundary target, and report comparisons rather than elapsed time alone.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Contiguous Layout has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: Define input, output, preconditions, size, and the operation being counted
